title: hippochaete laevigata var. polystachya
intro: Hippochaite laevigata var. polystachya, also known as the Horse Tail plant, is a type of fern that belongs to the Equisetum genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and relieving symptoms of colds and flu. Hippochaite laevigata var. polystachya can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
